title: A Tendon-Driven Wrist Abduction-Adduction Joint Improves Performance of a 5 DoF Upper Limb Exoskeleton -- Implementation and Experimental Evaluation
abstract: 手腕功能对于完成日常生活活动至关重要。然而，目前关于上肢外骨骼在康复中提供手腕外展-内收关节辅助功能影响的实验证据有限。本研究评估了在五自由度上肢外骨骼EXOTIC2中引入主动式手腕外展-内收关节对重度运动功能障碍患者的支持效果。方法：将采用肌腱驱动外展与弹簧驱动内收的紧凑轻量化手腕模块集成至EXOTIC外骨骼中。八名无运动功能障碍的成年受试者分别在随机设定的手腕活动锁定与解锁条件下完成饮水与搔抓任务，并对一名肌萎缩侧索硬化症患者进行了初步可行性测试。通过运动学参数与任务表现指标评估手腕活动范围、任务完成时间、液体泼溅量及表面平整度等指标。结果：集成手腕外展-内收自由度显著提升任务成功指标。饮水任务中的液体泼溅发生率从56%降至3%，搔抓任务中的表面平整度成功率从28%提升至75%。结论：集成手腕外展-内收辅助功能可在不增加执行时间的前提下改善关键功能性任务表现。意义：本研究首次通过实验证据表明，主动式手腕外展-内收控制可提升外骨骼辅助下日常生活活动的任务级表现。
preview_text: Wrist function is essential in performing activities of daily living (ADLs). However, there is limited experimental evidence on the functional impact of wrist Abduction-Adduction (Ab-Ad) joint assistance in upper limb exoskeletons (ULEs) for rehabilitation. This study evaluates the effect of implementing an active wrist Ab-Ad joint in a five degree of freedom (DoF) ULE, EXOTIC2 exoskeleton, to support individuals with severe motor impairments. Methods: A compact, lightweight wrist module with tendon-driven abduction and spring-driven adduction was integrated into the EXOTIC exoskeleton. Eight adults with no motor disabilities completed drinking and scratching tasks under randomized wrist-enabled and wrist-locked conditions along with a preliminary feasibility test in one individual with Amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS). Kinematic and task performance metrics including wrist range of motion, task completion time, spillage and leveling metrics were assessed. Results: Implementing the wrist Ab-Ad DoF improved task success metrics. Spill incidence during the drinking task decreased from 56% to 3%, and leveling success for scratching task improved from 28% to 75%. Conclusion: Integrating wrist Ab-Ad assistance improved key functional task outcomes without increasing execution time. Significance: The study provides the experimental evidence that active wrist Ab-Ad control enhances task-level performance in exoskeleton-assisted ADLs.
one_line_summary: 该论文研究上肢外骨骼的腕关节辅助功能，与强化学习、VLA、运动控制等关键词无关。
